corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: يُوصِيكُمُ needs finite Form IV/causative review plus كُم object before an authored hover such as "He instructs you" can be certified
drill_prompt: Segment يُوصِيكُمُ and explain why "to instruct" is a dictionary gloss, not a token hover.
learner_explanation: يُوصِيكُمُ contains a verb meaning instructs/commands and the attached pronoun كُم, meaning you all.
nahw_lesson: The verse context supplies the command/instruction relation; the object suffix is visible and must affect the English.
sarf_lesson: Record imperfect verb form, subject agreement, and كُم object suffix before accepting a token hover.
visible_bad_hover: to instruct, command, advise, or leave a last will
what_failed: A finite verb with visible second-person plural object suffix inherited entry infinitive prose.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: تَبَرُّوهُمْ needs finite verb review and هُمْ object before a hover such as "act kindly toward them" can be certified
drill_prompt: Find the object pronoun in تَبَرُّوهُمْ and write the unsafe shortcut it blocks.
learner_explanation: The ending هُمْ means them. The hover must show that the action is directed toward them.
nahw_lesson: The object pronoun and clause relation decide "toward them"; do not ship a lemma gloss.
sarf_lesson: The verb host and هُمْ suffix must be segmented before the برّ root family can be used.
visible_bad_hover: to act kindly, vast in grace
what_failed: A root-family/entry phrase hid the attached object pronoun on a finite verb.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: ٱلْقَصَصِ is a definite/genitive noun in this slot; review as story/narrative before any entry repair
drill_prompt: Contrast نَقُصُّ and ٱلْقَصَصِ: which one is a finite verb and which one is a noun?
learner_explanation: ٱلْقَصَصِ has the article and noun shape; it should not teach the verb "to relate" as its token contribution.
nahw_lesson: The local construction decides whether the token contributes the story/narrative, not the act of narrating.
sarf_lesson: Article plus noun/case shape blocks a finite-verb gloss even when the root is ق ص ص.
visible_bad_hover: to relate a story, tracing events one after another
what_failed: A noun surface in a verb/root entry received finite/infinitive story-telling prose.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: غَالِبٌ is a nominal/active-participle surface and needs nominal role review before a hover such as "prevailing" or "one who prevails" can be certified
drill_prompt: Classify غَلَبَ, غَالِبٌ, and غُلِبَتِ by POS/voice before choosing a hover.
learner_explanation: غَالِبٌ is shaped like a doer/adjective, so the hover should not be the infinitive "to overcome".
nahw_lesson: The predicate or attribute role determines the English shape; the root meaning alone is not enough.
sarf_lesson: The فاعل pattern needs nominal-derivative handling, not finite verb wording.
what_failed: A nominal derivative inherited a verb infinitive from the غ ل ب entry family.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: فَرِجَالًا needs whole-token proof and context review; رِجَالًا may be "on foot" here, while أَرْجُل and رَجُل rows are different lemmas/shapes
drill_prompt: Sort رَجُلَيْنِ, رِجَالًا, فَرِجَالًا, and أَرْجُلِهِم by lemma and context role.
learner_explanation: The same root can produce different words. فَرِجَالًا cannot be fixed by the English word "legs" without exact context.
nahw_lesson: Context decides body-part noun versus travel-state/adverbial reading, and fā/wāw functions must be reviewed.
sarf_lesson: رَجُل, رِجَال, رِجْل, and أَرْجُل are not one surface-safe hover family.
what_failed: A root-key lane mixed man/men, leg(s), and on-foot readings and allowed a component-only row to look like a whole-token hover.
